name: Pydantic AI
tagline: A type-safe Python agent framework that brings the FastAPI feeling to building apps with LLMs
seoTitle: Pydantic AI - Type-Safe Python Agent Framework
metaDescription: Pydantic AI is a Python agent framework from the Pydantic team. Build LLM apps with validated, type-safe outputs across many models and providers.
overview: Pydantic AI is a Python agent framework built by the team behind Pydantic Validation, the validation layer used inside many popular LLM libraries and SDKs. It applies the same type-hint-driven, ergonomic design that FastAPI brought to web development to the job of building applications and workflows with generative AI.

It is aimed at Python developers who want predictable, type-checked results from language models instead of loosely structured text. You define an agent with a model and the structure you expect back, and Pydantic validates the model's output against your types, moving a class of errors from runtime to write-time.

Within the agent-frameworks category, Pydantic AI sits among general-purpose frameworks for building agents. It is model-agnostic, supporting a wide range of models and providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and many others), and integrates with Pydantic Logfire and OpenTelemetry for observability.
features: Type-safe by design: outputs are validated against your Python types, surfacing errors at write-time with full IDE auto-completion support.
Model-agnostic: works with most major models and providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Cohere, Mistral, and many more), with custom-model support when a provider is not built in.
Structured, streamed outputs: stream structured results continuously with validation as data is generated.
Built-in evals: systematically test and evaluate the accuracy of the agentic systems you build, and track performance over time.
Standards support: integrates Model Context Protocol (MCP), Agent2Agent (A2A), and UI event-stream standards for tools, interop, and interactive apps.
Observability: integrates with Pydantic Logfire and any OpenTelemetry-compatible backend for tracing, debugging, and cost tracking.

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package from PyPI, then define a minimal agent. The README's full quickstart is clipped, so the snippet below is a minimal, safe starting point based on the README's own Agent example; see the official docs for full details.

steps.heading: Install Pydantic AI
steps.body: Install from PyPI with pip. Pydantic AI is published as the pydantic-ai package.

steps.heading: Define and run a minimal agent
steps.body: Create an Agent with a model string, then run it. Set the relevant provider API key (here, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) in your environment first.
steps.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent

agent = Agent('anthropic:claude-opus-4-8')

result = agent.run_sync('What is Pydantic AI?')
print(result.output)

steps.heading: Read the documentation
steps.body: The full quickstart, model/provider setup, structured outputs, tools, and evals are documented at ai.pydantic.dev.

useCases: Extracting validated, structured data from language-model responses so downstream code can rely on typed fields instead of parsing free text.
Building production agents and multi-step workflows that need consistent, type-checked behavior across model and provider changes.
Writing model-agnostic LLM code that can switch between providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and others) without rewriting application logic.
Adding observability and evals to an agentic system to debug behavior and track accuracy and cost over time.
